headline: Wednesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
summary: The three major averages soared Tuesday on traders' hope that an end could be in sight for the war in Iran. The Dow Industrials jumped more than 1,100 points.

headline: Stocks are higher to start the month as optimism around Iran war ending grows: Live updates
summary: Stocks rose on Wednesday, while oil prices declined to start the month, as hope grew that an end to the U.S.-Iran war was on the horizon.

headline: Jim Cramer Says Conagra Brands’ High Dividend Is on “Historically an Unsustainable Level”
summary: Conagra Brands, Inc. (NYSE:CAG) was among the stocks Jim Cramer highlighted, along with his latest game plan as the oil-shock-driven sell-off continues. Cramer was bearish on the stock, as he said: Wednesday brings another report from another food stock that is hurting and that is Conagra. Now, here’s the stock that typifies what’s been happening […]

headline: Earnings To Watch: Conagra (CAG) Reports Q1 Results Tomorrow
summary: Packaged foods company Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) will be announcing earnings results tomorrow before market open. Here’s what to look for.

headline: Conagra Brands Announces Quarterly Dividend Payment
summary: Conagra Brands, Inc. (NYSE: CAG) today announced that its Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend payment of $0.35 per share of CAG common stock to be paid on June 3, 2026 to stockholders of record as of the close of business on April 30, 2026. Conagra Brands, Inc. has paid consecutive quarterly dividends since January 1976.

headline: McCormick buys Unilever's food business in deal that values it at nearly $45 billion
summary: McCormick will pay a combination of cash and equity to expand further into condiments and spreads.

headline: Chips Down, Ackman Up: How Iran, the Fed, and a Viral Post Defined Monday's Market
summary: Wall Street started the week with a whimper as memory chipmakers extended their brutal selloff on AI efficiency fears, crude oil pushed past $102, and Jerome Powell chose patience over panic.
